title: Pregnancy dating
text:
Our aim when dating a pregnancy is

to get the EDD (expected date of delivery)

Menstrual dating: getting the EDD from LMP
Ultrasound dating: getting EDD from US using fetal biometry

to calculate the current GA (conveniently from EDD, for easy comparison with US regardless of its GA)

Are there different types of “ages” for a pregnancy?

Menstrual age = GA: from the LMP
Conception age: from fertilization = menstrual – 2 weeks
Implantation age: from implantation = menstrual – 3 weeks

What is the EDD?

When calculating GA from an average 28-day cycle, EDD = 280 days (40 weeks) after LMP

For cycles longer than 28 days: add the difference from 28
For cycles shorter than 28 days: subtract the difference from 28

To get the EDD from the LMP (the 280 d = 40 w), we apply Naegele rule: \[EDD = LMP + 7d - 3m + 1y\] Tip: if LMP < April → \(EDD = LMP + 7d + 9m\)

Menstrual dating should be confirmed with US dating before 20 weeks.
Otherwise, this pregnancy is suboptimally dated.

EDD can be reassigned only once (if any) if the discrepancy threshold is met, according to the GA at US.

title: Antenatal fetal wellbeing
text:
According to GA

First Trimester (up to 13+6 weeks)

Ultrasound: Nuchal translucency, crown-rump length, early anatomy
Biochemical markers: PAPP-A, free beta-hCG
Cell-free fetal DNA testing
Chorionic villus sampling (CVS) if indicated
Fetal heartbeat detection
Dating scan
Early fetal anomaly screening
Assessment of multiple pregnancies
Early placental location assessment



Second Trimester (14 to 27+6 weeks)

Ultrasound: Detailed anatomy scan, growth assessment
Maternal serum screening: Quadruple test
Amniocentesis if indicated
Fetal echocardiography if indicated
Assessment of fetal movements
Placental location and function assessment
Cervical length measurement if indicated



Third Trimester (28 Weeks to delivery)

Ultrasound: Growth scans, amniotic fluid assessment, Doppler studies
Non-stress test (NST)
Biophysical profile (BPP)
Assessment of fetal position and presentation
Cervical assessment for labor readiness
Fetal movement counting
Assessment of placental function and aging
Monitoring for signs of fetal distress
Preparation for delivery planning
Assessment of multiple pregnancies
Evaluation of fetal well-being in high-risk pregnancies
Assessment of fetal growth restriction (FGR) or macrosomia
Screening for gestational diabetes effects on fetus
Monitoring for preeclampsia effects on fetus
Evaluation of amniotic fluid volume (polyhydramnios/oligohydramnios)
Fetal lung maturity assessment if preterm delivery is anticipated
